esp_bridge_outdated.title: ESP bridge firmware update required
esp_bridge_outdated.description:
Your ESP bridge is running **v{version}**, but **v{min_version}** or newer is required for full functionality.

Rebuild and flash your ESPHome device to get the latest firmware.

See the ESP Bridge Setup Guide in the integration documentation for details.

standalone_card_installed.title: Standalone Philips Shaver Card is still installed
standalone_card_installed.description:
The Philips Shaver Card now ships with this integration and no longer needs to be installed separately.

A standalone copy of the card is still registered as a dashboard resource (`{url}`). Both copies register the same card element and only one of them wins, so the standalone copy can silently shadow the bundled, up-to-date card.

Please remove the standalone installation:

1. If installed via HACS: open HACS, find *Philips Shaver Card* and uninstall it (this also removes the dashboard resource).
2. If added manually: delete the resource under *Settings → Dashboards → Resources* and remove the file from your `www` folder.

Your dashboards keep working unchanged — the bundled card uses the same `custom:philips-shaver-card` type.

data_description.notify_throttle_ms: Minimum interval between BLE notification events forwarded by the ESP bridge (in milliseconds). Lower values give faster updates but may overload the ESP API buffer. Only applies to ESP32 bridge connections.
data_description.pipelined_reads: Send the poll cycle's GATT reads to the ESP bridge as one batch instead of one at a time (much faster reconnects). Requires bridge firmware 1.10.0 or newer — on older firmware reads always stay sequential, regardless of this setting. Disable only if you see repeated read timeouts or ATT watchdog messages in the bridge logs. Only applies to ESP32 bridge connections.

user.title: Add Philips Shaver
user.description: Choose how to connect to your shaver.

user_bleak.title: Direct Bluetooth
user_bleak.description: Enter the Bluetooth MAC address of the shaver.

esp_bridge.title: ESP32 Bridge (ESPHome)
esp_bridge.description: Select the ESPHome device that acts as the BLE bridge for your shaver.

data_description.esp_device_name: Legend: ⚪ ESP unreachable  •  🔗 paired slot  •  🟢 empty slot

bluetooth_confirm.title: Confirm detected device
bluetooth_confirm.description:
{status}Do you want to add the detected Philips Shaver **{name}** to Home Assistant?

ℹ️ **Before continuing, please verify:**
1. ⬜ Shaver is **turned on** or on its **charging stand**
2. ⬜ Shaver is **unpaired** from your phone
   — Remove from Bluetooth settings **and** GroomTribe / OneBlade app

Pairing will be handled automatically during setup. The integration will then connect to the device to read hardware capabilities. This may take a few seconds.

esp_select_device.title: Select BLE Device
esp_select_device.description:
This ESP bridge has multiple Philips devices connected. Select the device you want to add.

✅ already configured · 🔒 bonded · 🔓 unpaired · 🟢 online · ⚪ offline

data_description.esp_bridge_id: Entries without status icons are empty bridges in pair-mode. Switch on the shaver you want to bond before submitting.

esp_bridge_status.title: Connection via ESP32 Bridge
esp_bridge_status.description:
Setting up **{shaver_name}** via **ESP32 Bridge**.

✅ Bridge **{target}** is reachable.

{status}

Make sure the shaver is **turned on** (press the power button or place it on its charging stand) and **keep it on**, then click **Read capabilities**.

esp_bridge_status_connected.title: Connection via ESP32 Bridge
esp_bridge_status_connected.description:
Setting up **{shaver_name}** via **ESP32 Bridge**.

✅ Bridge **{target}** is reachable.

{status}

The bridge is already connected to your shaver. **Keep it switched on** — click **Read capabilities** to finish setup.

request_pair.title: Pair shaver to ESP bridge
request_pair.description:
Bridge **{target}** has no shaver bonded yet.

1. Make sure the shaver is **turned on** and **not connected to your phone** (remove it from Bluetooth settings and the GroomTribe / OneBlade app).
2. Place the shaver close to the bridge.
3. Click **Start pairing** below — the bridge will scan for **60 seconds**. Trigger the shaver's BLE-advertise mode (model-specific: hold the power button or place it on the charging stand).

Once the bond is established, capabilities will be probed automatically.

pair.title: Pair Device
pair.description:
**{name}** needs to be paired with this system.

ℹ️ **Before pairing, please verify:**
1. ⬜ Shaver is **turned on** or on its **charging stand**
2. ⬜ Shaver is **not connected** to your phone
   — Remove from Bluetooth settings **and** GroomTribe / OneBlade app

not_paired.title: Pairing Required
not_paired.description:
**{name}** is not paired with this system.

{pairing_help}

`{pair_cmd}`

The script will scan for nearby Philips devices and guide you through the pairing process.

data_description.device_name: How this shaver appears in Home Assistant. Pick something that distinguishes it from any other shavers you have (e.g., "Bathroom", "John's").

error.no_esphome_devices: No ESPHome devices found. Please set up an ESPHome device with the BLE bridge component first. See the ESP Bridge Setup Guide in the integration documentation for instructions.
error.device_asleep: The shaver is asleep. Wake it (press the power button or place it on its charging stand), then try again.
error.cannot_connect: Failed to connect to the shaver. Please ensure it is powered on, in range, and not connected to another device (e.g. your phone).
error.device_not_found: Device not found. Please ensure it is powered on and in range.
error.not_paired: The shaver is not paired with this system.
error.out_of_slots: No Bluetooth adapter could reach the device. Either all connection slots are in use, or the device is out of range. Ensure the shaver is powered on and close to the Bluetooth adapter, and try removing unused Bluetooth devices if needed.
error.connection_aborted: Connection was aborted. The device may be out of range or connected through an unsupported Bluetooth proxy. This integration requires direct Bluetooth or the dedicated ESP32 BLE bridge — standard ESPHome Bluetooth Proxies are not supported.
error.pairing_failed: Bluetooth pairing failed. Ensure the shaver is powered on and not connected to another device. If the problem persists, try turning the shaver off and back on, or pair manually via terminal.
error.service_call_failed: Could not arm pair-mode on the ESP bridge. The bridge may be offline or a too-old firmware version. Update the bridge to v1.8.0 or newer and retry.
error.pair_timeout: No shaver appeared during the 60-second pair-mode window. Make sure the shaver is on and advertising, and try again.
error.pair_failed_stale_bond: Pairing failed with repeated auth errors. The shaver still holds the previous bond on its side and is rejecting fresh pairing. Clear Bluetooth on the shaver (model-specific: long-press the power button until the BT LED clears, or unpair via the GroomTribe / OneBlade app), then retry.
error.unknown: Unexpected error. Please check the logs.

abort.already_in_progress: Configuration flow is already in progress.
abort.no_devices_found: No supported Philips Shaver devices were found in range.
abort.esp_not_reachable: The ESP bridge is not reachable. Make sure the ESP is powered on and connected to your network, then retry.
